1: A shadow: "Unless the LORD builds the house, those who build it labor in vain." Matthew 16:18 — "I will build my church, and the gates of hell shall not prevail against it." 1 Corinthians 3:10-11 — "I laid a foundation, and someone else is building upon it. Let each one take care how he builds upon it. For no one can lay a foundation other than that which is laid, which is Jesus Christ." All human effort to build what God must build is vanity; Christ is both the foundation and the builder.
2: A theme: "he gives sleep to his beloved." Matthew 11:28-30 — "Come to me, all who labor and are heavy laden, and I will give you rest." The anxious toil of self-sufficiency (rising early, staying up late) stands against the rest of those who trust the builder. Christ himself demonstrated this rest — sleeping undisturbed in the storm (Mark 4:38), the one who gives rest embodying it perfectly.
3: A theme: "children are a heritage from the LORD, the fruit of the womb a reward." Those born through Christ are his heritage and offspring. John 1:12-13 — those who receive Christ are born "not of blood nor of the will of the flesh nor of the will of man, but of God." Isaiah 53:10 — "he shall see his offspring and prolong his days." The spiritual children born through Christ's work are his reward and inheritance.
4: A theme: "Like arrows in the hand of a warrior are the children of one's youth." The expansion of Christ's family through the proclamation of the gospel — disciples sent as arrows into all nations (Matthew 28:19-20; John 12:32 — "I, when I am lifted up from the earth, will draw all people to myself"). The quiver-filling is the gathering of the elect from every nation (Revelation 7:9).
5: A theme: "Blessed is the man who fills his quiver with them! He shall not be put to shame when he speaks with his enemies in the gate." Christ speaks before all his enemies with final authority — Philippians 2:10-11 (every knee bows at his name). The one who builds the house, provides for the household, and fills his quiver is the victor who faces no shame. Hebrews 2:9 — Christ is "crowned with glory and honor because of the suffering of death."
